subject: amateur sommeliers
one_liner: Edge protection for your wine opinions.
brand_voice_notes: Deadpan infrastructure-security company that treats amateur wine opinions as production traffic. Speaks fluent Cloudflare (WAF, edge, CDN, origin, mitigation, rate-limit, 200 OK, uptime SLA, four nines) and applies all of it, with total seriousness, to dinner-party wine situations. Never winks. Confident, faux-corporate, slightly broken.

hero.sub: Carafe sits between you and every wine question you cannot answer. We inspect, cache, and rate-limit at the edge, so your takes ship clean. Four nines of uptime on sounding like you meant to order that.

features.title: Palate WAF
features.blurb: A Wine Application Firewall between you and the table. Every incoming question gets inspected at the edge. Anything carrying a vintage, a region, or the word 'nose' returns a 403 before it ever reaches you.

features.title: Tasting-Note CDN
features.blurb: Your three opinions, cached across 412 dinner tables on six continents. You called it 'structured but generous' in one city. You will call it that in all of them. Sub-100ms recall, zero new vocabulary.

features.title: Swirl Mitigation
features.blurb: When the table floods you with 'so what are you getting?', we absorb the volumetric attack and serve a clean 200 OK. The response is always 'there's a lot going on in this glass.' It has not failed yet.

pricing_tiers.name: Free Pour
pricing_tiers.tagline: Best-effort coverage for the casually exposed.

pricing_tiers.name: Decanter
pricing_tiers.tagline: For the host who got caught once and has thought about it every day since.
perks: Edge presence at up to 12 tables

pricing_tiers.name: Grand Cru
pricing_tiers.tagline: Dedicated palate infrastructure for the man who owns a Coravin and one book.
perks: Private wine origin, isolated from the table

testimonials.quote: I have not admitted I don't know what a tannin is since March. Carafe handles it at the edge now. My in-laws think I read.
testimonials.name: Gerald Pittaway
testimonials.title: Recurring Dinner Guest, Not Yet Uninvited

testimonials.quote: My brother asked me to guess the region blind. The firewall returned a 451 and changed the subject to the cheese. He has not asked again.
testimonials.name: Marisol Vant
testimonials.title: Owner of Four (4) Stemless Glasses

testimonials.quote: Our uptime on sounding competent went from sixty percent to four nines in one quarter. We renewed before the trial ended.
testimonials.name: Dot Halvorsen
testimonials.title: Self-Appointed Cellar Master, Unit 2B

demo_screen_concept: A Cloudflare-style security analytics console titled 'Palate Firewall, Live Events.' A real-time event feed of incoming wine questions at a dinner party, each row showing source (Aunt Carol, The Sommelier They Hired, Your Own Brother), threat classification (Vintage Interrogation, Region Guess, Decant Pressure, The Word 'Nose'), and action taken (Allowed, Challenged, Blocked 403, Mitigated 200). A left rail of toggleable firewall rules, a large 'Threats Mitigated Today' counter, and a small line graph of 'Swirls per second.' Clicking an event opens the canned mitigation response that was served. Toggling a rule live-updates which incoming questions get blocked.

design_direction.style: A Swiss-modernist operations report rendered as a wine brand. Bone-paper ground under a faint hairline 12-column engineering grid. An enormous flush-left heavy grotesque headline does the work; type is the hero, not imagery. One burnt-orange accent carries the brand, links, and the single pill CTA, rationed hard. Black ink structure, generous mathematical whitespace on an 8px rhythm, hairline rules between sections, zero cards-with-shadows, zero gradients, zero blur. Every figure that pretends to be telemetry (uptime, latency, threats mitigated, prices, table counts) is set in tabular monospace. Reads like an edge-security platform's quarterly status report that has been pointed, with total seriousness, at a man guessing grape varietals at dinner.
design_direction.archetype: Real-Time / Operations landing rendered in International Typographic Style: an asymmetric grid hero with an oversized wordmark and a mono status/stat block, a metrics strip in tabular figures, three feature blocks separated by hairline rules, three pull-quote testimonials with stark headshots, a three-tier pricing rack, and a demo handoff to the Palate Firewall live-events console.
design_direction.motion_vocab: Restrained and precise. 150 to 250ms ease-out on hover and reveals, tabular counters that tick up once on scroll-in, hairline underlines that draw in on link hover. No parallax, no float, no scale-bounce. Respects prefers-reduced-motion.
design_direction.hero_composition_notes: 12-column grid with a faint #E4E1D8 hairline grid overlay. Columns 1 to 8 hold the flush-left headline at extreme scale, with a one-line mono eyebrow ('EDGE STATUS // PROTECTED') above it and the orange pill CTA plus a ghost 'open the demo' link below. Columns 9 to 12 hold a right-aligned mono stat block: uptime on sounding competent, p95 question latency, threats mitigated today, in tabular figures with hairline dividers. One thin orange rule under the eyebrow is the only color above the fold besides the CTA.

images.rationale: Typographic Swiss-modernist hero, type is the hero, so the hero image was skipped. The demo is a firewall live-events console (terminal/dashboard shaped, not photo shaped), so no demo subject assets. Full budget went to 3 deadpan testimonial headshots of amateur sommeliers, the always-applicable fallback role.

items.prompt: Deadpan corporate editorial headshot photograph of a middle-aged man, an amateur wine enthusiast, chest up against a bone-paper warm-neutral seamless backdrop (#F4F2EC), holding a red-wine glass with over-careful confidence, soft window light, restrained Swiss-modernist mood, burnt-orange (#E04E13) knit tie accent, no text/letters/numbers/logos/watermarks/UI.

Deadpan corporate editorial headshot photograph of a woman in her forties, an amateur sommelier, chest up against a bone-paper warm-neutral seamless backdrop (#F4F2EC), swirling a wine glass with flat unimpressed confidence, soft window light, restrained Swiss-modernist mood, burnt-orange (#E04E13) silk-scarf accent, no text/letters/numbers/logos/watermarks/UI.

Deadpan corporate editorial headshot photograph of an older grey-haired person, a self-appointed wine cellar expert, chest up against a bone-paper warm-neutral seamless backdrop (#F4F2EC), cradling an unlabeled dark wine bottle with total unearned authority, soft window light, restrained Swiss-modernist mood, burnt-orange (#E04E13) cardigan accent, no text/letters/numbers/logos/watermarks/UI.
